en: Then Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples,
zh: 那時，耶穌對群眾和他的門徒說：

en: "The scribes and the Pharisees sit on Moses' seat,
zh: “經學家和法利賽人坐在摩西的座位上，

en: so practice and observe whatever they tell you- but not what they do. For they preach, but do not practice.
zh: 所以凡他們吩咐你們的，你們都要遵行和謹守，但不可效法他們的行為，因為他們只會說而不去作。

en: They tie up heavy burdens, hard to bear, and lay them on people's shoulders, but they themselves are not willing to move them with their finger.
zh: 他們把重擔捆起來，壓在人的肩頭上，但自己連一個指頭也不肯動。

en: They do all their deeds to be seen by others. For they make their phylacteries broad and their fringes long,
zh: 他們所作的一切，都是要作給人看：他們把經文的匣子做大了，衣服的繸子做長了；

en: and they love the place of honor at feasts and the best seats in the synagogues
zh: 他們喜歡筵席上的首位、會堂裡的高位；

en: and greetings in the marketplaces and being called rabbi by others.
zh: 又喜歡人在市中心向他們問安，稱呼他們‘拉比’。

en: But you are not to be called rabbi, for you have one teacher, and you are all brothers.
zh: 然而你們不要被人稱為‘拉比’，因為只有一位是你們的老師，你們都是弟兄。

en: And call no man your father on earth, for you have one Father, who is in heaven.
zh: 不要稱呼地上的人為父，因為只有一位是你們的父，就是天父。

en: Neither be called instructors, for you have one instructor, the Christ.
zh: 你們也不要被人稱為師傅，因為只有一位是你們的師傅，就是基督。

en: The greatest among you shall be your servant.
zh: 你們中間最大的，必作你們的僕人。

en: Whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.
zh: 凡高抬自己的，必被降卑；凡自己謙卑的，必被升高。

en: "But woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you shut the kingdom of heaven in people's faces. For you neither enter yourselves nor allow those who would enter to go in.
zh: “虛偽的經學家和法利賽人哪，你們有禍了！你們在人面前關了天國的門，自己不進去，連正要進去的人，你們也不准他們進去。

en: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you travel across sea and land to make a single proselyte, and when he becomes a proselyte, you make him twice as much a child of hell as yourselves.
zh: “虛偽的經學家和法利賽人哪，你們有禍了！你們走遍海洋陸地，要使一個人入教；當他入了教，你們卻使他淪為地獄之子，比你們更甚。

en: "Woe to you, blind guides, who say, 'If anyone swears by the temple, it is nothing, but if anyone swears by the gold of the temple, he is bound by his oath.'
zh: “你們這些瞎眼的嚮導有禍了！你們說：‘凡指著聖所而起的誓，是沒有用的；但指著聖所的金子而起的誓，就必須遵守。’

en: You blind fools! For which is greater, the gold or the temple that has made the gold sacred?
zh: 你們這些瞎眼的愚昧人哪，到底是金子大，還是使金子成聖的聖所大呢？

en: And you say, 'If anyone swears by the altar, it is nothing, but if anyone swears by the gift that is on the altar, he is bound by his oath.'
zh: 你們又說：‘凡指著祭壇所起的誓，是沒有用的；但指著壇上的祭物所起的誓，就必須遵守。’

en: You blind men! For which is greater, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
zh: 瞎眼的人哪！到底是祭物大，還是使祭物成聖的祭壇大呢？

en: So whoever swears by the altar swears by it and by everything on it.
zh: 所以，凡指著祭壇起誓的，他就是指著祭壇和壇上的一切起誓；

en: And whoever swears by the temple swears by it and by him who dwells in it.
zh: 指著聖所起誓的，就是指著聖所和住在那裡的 神起誓；

en: And whoever swears by heaven swears by the throne of God and by him who sits upon it.
zh: 指著天起誓的，就是指著 神的寶座和坐在寶座上的起誓。

en: "Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you tithe mint and dill and cumin, and have neglected the weightier matters of the law: justice and mercy and faithfulness. These you ought to have done, without neglecting the others.
zh: “虛偽的經學家和法利賽人哪，你們有禍了！你們把薄荷、茴香、芹菜，獻上十分之一，卻忽略律法上更重要的，就如正義、憐憫和信實；這些更重要的是你們應當作的，但其他的也不可忽略。

en: You blind guides, straining out a gnat and swallowing a camel!
zh: 你們這些瞎眼的嚮導啊，你們把蚊蟲濾出來，卻把駱駝吞下去。

en: "Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you clean the outside of the cup and the plate, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.
zh: “虛偽的經學家和法利賽人哪，你們有禍了！你們洗淨杯盤的外面，裡面卻裝滿了搶奪和放蕩。

en: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup and the plate, that the outside also may be clean.
zh: 瞎眼的法利賽人哪，先把杯和盤的裡面洗淨，好使外面也可以乾淨。

en: "Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like whitewashed tombs, which outwardly appear beautiful, but within are full of dead people's bones and all uncleanness.
zh: “虛偽的經學家和法利賽人哪，你們有禍了！你們好像粉飾了的墳墓，外面好看，裡面卻裝滿了死人的骨頭和各樣的污穢；

en: So you also outwardly appear righteous to others, but within you are full of hypocrisy and lawlessness.
zh: 照樣，你們外面看來像義人，裡面卻充塞著虛偽和不法。

en: "Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you build the tombs of the prophets and decorate the monuments of the righteous,
zh: “虛偽的經學家和法利賽人哪，你們有禍了！你們建造先知的墓，修飾義人的碑，

en: saying, 'If we had lived in the days of our fathers, we would not have taken part with them in shedding the blood of the prophets.'
zh: 並且說：‘我們若活在我們祖先的時代，決不會與他們一同流先知的血。’

en: Thus you witness against yourselves that you are sons of those who murdered the prophets.
zh: 這樣，你們就指證自己是殺害先知的人的子孫了。

en: Fill up, then, the measure of your fathers.
zh: 那麼，去完成你們祖先的罪孽吧！

en: You serpents, you brood of vipers, how are you to escape being sentenced to hell?
zh: 你們這些蛇，這些毒蛇所生的啊，你們怎能逃脫地獄的刑罰呢？

en: Therefore I send you prophets and wise men and scribes, some of whom you will kill and crucify, and some you will flog in your synagogues and persecute from town to town,
zh: 因此，我差派先知、智慧人和經學家到你們那裡；有些你們要殺害又釘在十字架上，有些你們要在會堂裡鞭打，從一個城趕逐到另一個城。

en: so that on you may come all the righteous blood shed on earth, from the blood of innocent Abel to the blood of Zechariah the son of Barachiah, whom you murdered between the sanctuary and the altar.
zh: 所以，從義人亞伯的血起，到巴拉加的兒子撒迦利亞的血為止，他就是你們在聖所和祭壇中間所殺的，所有義人在地上所流的血，都要歸在你們身上。

en: Truly, I say to you, all these things will come upon this generation.
zh: 我實在告訴你們，這一切都必臨到這個世代。

en: "O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, the city that kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to it! How often would I have gathered your children together as a hen gathers her brood under her wings, and you would not!
zh: “耶路撒冷，耶路撒冷啊，你殺害先知，又用石頭把奉派到你們那裡的人打死。我多次想招聚你的兒女，好像母雞招聚小雞到翅膀底下，只是你們不願意。

en: See, your house is left to you desolate.
zh: 你看，你們的家必成為荒場，留給你們。

en: For I tell you, you will not see me again, until you say, 'Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.'"
zh: 我告訴你們，從今以後，你們一定見不到我，直等到你們說：‘奉主名來的，是應當稱頌的。’”
